steps.title: Fit the two function values
steps.content: Use the given values as points: $f(2)=3$ means $(2,3)$ and $f(6)=5$ means $(6,5)$. In Desmos, enter $x_1=[2,6]$, $y_1=[3,5]$, and $y_1\sim mx_1+b$. Desmos gives $m=\frac{1}{2}$ and $b=2$.

steps.title: Check the equation
steps.content: By hand, $m=\frac{5-3}{6-2}=\frac{1}{2}$. Substitute $(2,3)$ into $y=mx+b$: $3=\frac{1}{2}(2)+b$, so $b=2$. Thus $f(x)=\frac{1}{2}x+2$.

steps.title: Eliminate the answer choices
steps.content:
Test each at $x=2$ and $x=6$:
  • A) $f(x)=-2x+17$: $f(2)=13$, not $3$. Fails.
  • B) $f(x)=-2x+15$: $f(2)=11$, not $3$. Fails.
  • C) $f(x)=\tfrac{1}{2}x+2$: $f(2)=3$ and $f(6)=5$. Both check.
  • D) $f(x)=\tfrac{1}{2}x+14$: $f(2)=15$, not $3$. Fails.
That matches choice C — C is correct.
